---
title: "atspR — Getting Started"
subtitle: "Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R"
date: "v1.0.0 · MIT License · EasternTechFusion/atspR"
output:
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    highlight: tango
    toc: true
    toc_float:
      collapsed: false
      smooth_scroll: true
    toc_depth: 3
    self_contained: true
    css: style.css
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, eval = FALSE)
```

---

## Overview

`atspR` provides an automated, modular preprocessing pipeline for time-series data — covering the full journey from raw sensor data to cross-validated, model-ready train and test sets.

Designed for students and domain practitioners (agronomists, environmental scientists, irrigation engineers) who need a reliable, leakage-free preprocessing workflow without managing multiple packages or worrying about step sequencing.

| Problem | How `atspR` solves it |
|---|---|
| Scalers applied before splitting → **data leakage** | Scaler fitted on **TRAIN only**, applied to both sets |
| Random split invalidates time-series models | **Temporal split** — train always precedes test |
| Silent NA removal with no record | Full report of what was dropped or imputed |
| Multi-package workflow, easy to sequence incorrectly | Single `ts_preprocess()` runs all steps in order |
| Error messages with no guidance | Every decision explained in plain-language output |

---

## Installation

```r
# Install devtools if needed
# install.packages("devtools")

devtools::install_github("EasternTechFusion/atspR")
library(atspR)
```

---

## Pipeline

The full pipeline runs 10 stages in sequence:

```
Raw data
   │
   ├─ (Stage 1)  combine_datetime()   [optional] Merge date + time → POSIXct
   ├─ (Stage 2)  fill_time_gaps()     Insert placeholder rows for missing timestamps
   │
   └─ ts_preprocess() ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
        (Stage 3)  standardize_na()   Convert sentinel values to NA
        (Stage 4)  coerce_numeric()   Parse character columns to numeric
        (Stage 5)  split_data()       Temporal train/test split
        (Stage 6)  missing_analysis() Summarise NAs; decide DROP or IMPUTE
        (Stage 7)  handle_missing()   Drop rows or interpolate (linear/KNN)
        (Stage 8)  visualize_data()   Scatter plots per variable vs time
        (Stage 9)  scale_data()       MinMax / Z-score / Robust (auto)
        (Stage 10) cross_validate()   [optional] Walk-forward k-fold CV
   │
   └─ ts_export()  Write all outputs to CSV
```

---

## Quick Start

### Case 1: Date + Time in separate columns {#case1}

**Sample input:**

| Date | Time | Temp | Humid | Solar | WindDirection | WindSpeed | RainFall | VPD |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2024-01-01 | 08:00 | 25.12 | 80.04 | 14.06 | 94.82 | 0.00 | 0.00 | 0.2758 |
| 2024-01-01 | 09:00 | NA | 82.38 | 18.33 | 91.23 | 0.52 | 0.00 | 0.3124 |
| 2024-01-01 | 11:00 | 26.26 | 85.03 | 22.78 | 88.65 | 1.14 | 0.00 | 0.3892 |

> Hour 10:00 is missing entirely — `fill_time_gaps()` will insert it as a row of `NA`.

```r
library(atspR)

# Step 1 — merge date and time columns into a single POSIXct column
df <- combine_datetime(my_data,
                       date_col  = "Date",
                       time_col  = "Time",
                       new_col   = "datetime",
                       time_type = "string")

# Step 2 — fill missing timestamps with NA rows
gap <- fill_time_gaps(df,
                      time_col = "datetime",
                      n        = 1,
                      unit     = "hour")

# Step 3 — run the full preprocessing pipeline
result <- ts_preprocess(data        = gap$data,
                        train_ratio = 0.8,
                        target_col  = "VPD",
                        k_folds     = 5)
```

---

### Case 2: Datetime in one column {#case2}

**Sample input:**

| DateTime | Temp | Humid | Solar | VPD |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2024-01-01 08:00 | 25.12 | 80.04 | 14.06 | 0.2758 |
| 2024-01-01 09:00 | NA | 82.38 | 18.33 | 0.3124 |
| 2024-01-01 11:00 | 26.26 | 85.03 | 22.78 | 0.3892 |

```r
library(atspR)

# Parse the datetime column to POSIXct
df$datetime <- as.POSIXct(df$datetime,
                           format = "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")

# Fill missing timestamps
gap <- fill_time_gaps(df,
                      time_col = "datetime",
                      n        = 1,
                      unit     = "hour")

# Run pipeline
result <- ts_preprocess(data        = gap$data,
                        train_ratio = 0.8,
                        target_col  = "VPD",
                        k_folds     = 5)
```

---

### Case 3: Date only (daily data) {#case3}

**Sample input:**

| date | Temp | Humid | Solar | VPD |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 2024-01-01 | 28.38 | 75.04 | 14.06 | 0.2758 |
| 2024-01-02 | 29.16 | 72.05 | NA | 0.3124 |
| 2024-01-04 | 27.89 | 78.03 | 22.78 | 0.3892 |

> 2024-01-03 is missing — `fill_time_gaps()` will insert it as a row of `NA`.

```r
library(atspR)

# Fill missing dates
gap <- fill_time_gaps(df,
                      time_col = "date",
                      n        = 1,
                      unit     = "day")

# Run pipeline
result <- ts_preprocess(data        = gap$data,
                        train_ratio = 0.8,
                        target_col  = "VPD",
                        k_folds     = 5)
```

---

## `ts_preprocess()` Parameters

| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `data` | data.frame | — | Input dataframe (after `fill_time_gaps()`) |
| `target_col` | character | — | Name of the response variable column |
| `train_ratio` | numeric [0–1] | `0.8` | Proportion of data used for training |
| `k_folds` | integer | `5` | Number of walk-forward CV folds; `NULL` to skip |
| `impute_method` | character | `"linear"` | `"linear"` or `"knn"` interpolation |
| `scale_method` | character | `"auto"` | `"minmax"`, `"zscore"`, `"robust"`, or `"auto"` |

> **`scale_method = "auto"`** selects robust scaling for skewed distributions, and MinMax otherwise.

---

## Export Results

```r
ts_export(result, dir = "C:/Users/user/", prefix = "data")
```

This writes four files to the specified directory:

| File | Contents |
|---|---|
| `data_train.csv` | Scaled training set |
| `data_test.csv` | Scaled test set |
| `data_cv_folds.csv` | Walk-forward CV fold assignments |
| `data_preprocessing_report.csv` | Full log of every automated decision |

---

## Cross-Validation

`atspR` uses **walk-forward validation** — the correct approach for time-series data. Each validation fold is preceded only by past data, so no future information leaks into training.

```
Seed(20%)  Fold1  Fold2  Fold3  Fold4  Fold5
[─────────][─────][─────][─────][─────][─────]

Fold 1: train = seed              → val = fold1
Fold 2: train = seed + fold1      → val = fold2
Fold 3: train = seed + fold1 + 2  → val = fold3
Fold 4: train = seed + fold1–3    → val = fold4
Fold 5: train = seed + fold1–4    → val = fold5
```

> **Why this matters:** The scaler is fitted on the training set only and applied to both train and test sets — eliminating data leakage. The temporal split ensures the test set always follows the training set chronologically.

---

## Citation

```
Sueppong Mueanchamnong and Pattharaporn Thongnim (2026).
atspR: Automated Time-Series Preprocessing in R.
R package version 1.0.0.
https://github.com/EasternTechFusion/atspR
```

---

*MIT © 2024 · [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/EasternTechFusion/atspR)*
